The Friday Note

The $86 Billion Week

Issue #7 · March 21, 2026

This week felt like the starting gun went off. Salesforce bet $25 billion on Agentforce. Amazon committed $50 billion to OpenAI. IBM closed its $11 billion Confluent deal. Adobe partnered with NVIDIA on agentic workflows. And GPT-5.4 started clicking through websites by itself.

The message is clear: the infrastructure for autonomous marketing isn't coming—it's here. The question now is whether your team is building on it or being disrupted by it.

— Chris

Top Stories

The Five That Mattered

Salesforce

Salesforce Bets $25B on Agentforce

Record stock buyback funded by debt, but the real signal is rapid enterprise adoption—including a multi-year global deal with Adecco for "Agentforce 360."

OpenAI

GPT-5.4 Gets "Computer-Use" Skills

OpenAI's new model can navigate UIs, fill forms, and manipulate documents autonomously. 83% accuracy on real-world job tasks.

Adobe + NVIDIA

Agentic Workflows for Marketing

Joint initiative to build AI systems that autonomously handle content creation through campaign execution. Firefly + Omniverse.

IBM

IBM Closes $11B Confluent Acquisition

Real-time data streaming for AI models. 40% of Fortune 500 already use Confluent. Agentic AI needs live data, not batch.

NVIDIA GTC

Enterprise AI Agent Platform Goes Live

NVIDIA launched its AI agent platform with Adobe, Salesforce, and SAP as launch partners.

Stat of the Week

47%

Higher margins for AI-mature enterprises

Source: Salsify AI Maturity Report, March 2026

The gap between AI leaders and laggards isn't closing—it's widening. The competitive advantage window is now.

Quote of the Week

"We will all become AI composers, whether you're a marketer, programmer, or PM."

— Chris Hay, IBM Distinguished Engineer

Not "users" of AI. Not "prompters." Composers—orchestrating multiple AI systems to produce outcomes. That's the skill to develop.

The Bottom Line

What This Means for PMMs

1. Agents are the new apps. Salesforce's $25B bet isn't about CRM—it's about replacing point solutions with autonomous agents.

2. Computer-use changes everything. When AI can navigate interfaces, any app with a UI becomes automatable.

3. Real-time data becomes mandatory. IBM bought Confluent because agentic AI needs live signals, not batch jobs.

4. The hybrid model wins. 79% of execs run AI agents, but rule-based automation still handles sequences. The future is AI orchestrating both.
